query: HarmonyOS ArkTS/ArkUI platform fact check. API level: 22. Stage goal: verify exact local API usage while implementing. Focus: Whether the ArkUI page lifecycle hook onPageShow fires when returning to a page via router.back() (back-navigation), so a per-page refresh can re-snapshot shared singleton repository state. Task constraint: Cross-page match-count sync: when the user adds/removes a pantry item on the Pantry page (or clears pantry on Settings, or toggles a favorite on a recipe card) and then navigates back, the Discover/Search/Saved/RecipeDetail pages must re-snapshot the shared repository singletons and recompute match counts. Plan mandates each consuming page call viewModel.refresh() in onPageShow.. Project evidence: Pages are @Entry @Component structs navigated via router.pushUrl/router.back() (HarmonyOS router API). Each page binds an @Observed ViewModel. There is no shared reactive store; instead plan introduces singleton repositories (PantryRepository/FavoritesRepository) whose snapshot() must be re-read on page re-entry. Need to confirm onPageShow is the correct lifecycle hook for re-entry refresh on router.back(), and whether aboutToAppear also fires on back (it should not).. Platform surfaces: ArkUI page lifecycle (aboutToAppear/aboutToDisappear/onPageShow/onPageHide); router.back() navigation; @Entry page reactivation. Extra constraints: Offline only. Confirm onPageShow is invoked on router.back() re-entry to a previously-pushed page, and that aboutToAppear is NOT re-invoked on back. If onPageShow does not fire on back-nav, identify the correct alternative hook.. Check only: exact import/module/type/member/signature and minimal call shape; API-level availability for this call; required permission/config/context; return/error/null/default/conflict semantics; call-site lifecycle/threading constraints; compile/runtime failure traps. Quality bar: a coder can write or check the local code from the answer: exact import/module/type/member/signature, minimal call shape, context/permission/config, lifecycle/threading, return/error/null/default/conflict semantics, forbidden local usage, and validation check. Return concise evidence bullets only. Use these slots when applicable: exact_api, exact_import, return_error, context_permission, lifecycle_threading, local_forbidden, local_validation, planned_fallback. For critical slots, write `slot: none` when the fact is not applicable or not known. If sources disagree or the fact is unavailable, state the conflict or unknown explicitly; do not smooth it over. You may add extra:<name> if a critical fact does not fit. Do not invent missing facts. Max 8 bullets. Format each bullet as '- slot: fact'. No preface, headings, examples, tutorials, or steps.
